title: RapidMeta Neurology | Efgartigimod for Generalized Myasthenia Gravis v1.0
hero_h2: Efgartigimod in Anti-AChR-Positive Generalized Myasthenia Gravis

pico.pop: Adults with generalized myasthenia gravis (MGFA Class II-IV), anti-AChR antibody-positive, MG-ADL >=5 with at least 50% of points from non-ocular items, on stable background MG therapy
pico.int: Efgartigimod alfa 10 mg/kg IV weekly for 4 weeks (cycle), with subsequent cycles individualised by clinical response (neonatal Fc-receptor blocker, accelerates IgG catabolism)
pico.comp: Placebo-matching weekly IV infusions for 4 weeks per cycle on top of stable background therapy
pico.out: Proportion of MG-ADL responders (>=2-point reduction sustained for >=4 consecutive weeks during cycle 1) in anti-AChR-positive population (primary)
pico.subgroup: Background MG therapy (steroid only vs steroid+ISA), MGFA class, baseline MG-ADL severity, age

real_data_entries.name: ADAPT
real_data_entries.group: Anti-AChR+ generalized MG, efgartigimod 10 mg/kg IV weekly for 4 weeks vs placebo (cycle 1 MG-ADL responder primary)

allOutcomes.title: MG-ADL responder rate in cycle 1 (RR vs placebo; primary)
QMG responder rate in cycle 1 (RR vs placebo)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Howard JF Jr et al. Lancet Neurol 2021;20:526-536 (ADAPT).
